name: TDD-STATE-MACHINE-BACKLOG-2
title: SKILL.md edits via feature-touch must use skill-creator; prohibit direct feature file edits and manual scope markers
description:
When rabbit-feature-touch is invoked for a feature whose scope includes a SKILL.md edit, the workflow must route through /skill-creator:skill-creator — not edit the source SKILL.md directly.

Three rules:
1. SKILL.md edits go through skill-creator: invoke Skill(skill-creator:skill-creator) and let it drive skill authoring. Direct edits bypass its eval loop and description optimization.
2. No direct feature file edits: the main session orchestrator must never use Write or Edit tools on files inside .claude/features/. That is the TDD subagent's job. Main session role is orchestration only.
3. No manual scope marker writes: the main session must never create .rabbit-scope-active or .rabbit-scope-active-<feature> markers. Scope markers are the TDD subagent's responsibility at LOCK step.

Observed violation: 2026-05-17 session (PR #93) — main session manually created scope markers, directly edited SKILL.md source files with Edit tool, bypassing rabbit-feature-touch, rabbit-spec, and TDD subagent entirely.

Implementation: detect SKILL.md paths in the impl-suggestion affected_files list at Step 4 (Human Approval) and branch to skill-creator before dispatching the TDD subagent. Add manual scope marker creation to the Red Flags section of rabbit-feature-touch.

history.note: TDD cycle complete. (1) Assembled TDD subagent prompt now routes SKILL.md edits through Skill(skill-creator:skill-creator) in IMPLEMENT step. (2) rabbit-feature-touch Red Flags now prohibits main-session Write/Edit on .claude/features/. (3) Red Flags prohibits main-session .rabbit-scope-active* marker creation. Scoped to tdd-subagent (related_feature tdd-state-machine was stale). 24 tests pass.
